relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Two spies enter Jericho and find shelter in Rahab's house.
The king's intelligence reaches her doorway quickly.
Royal power demands that she surrender the hidden men.
Rahab protects them and controls the story told to the king.
She sends the pursuers into darkness along a false trail.
The spies lie beneath flax prepared upon her roof.
The gate closes while the pursuit runs toward Jordan.
Rahab climbs to negotiate before the men sleep.
She names the terror already spreading through the city.
Exodus and destroyed kings have become the reports shaping Jericho's fear.
Rahab's conclusion reaches beyond terror: YHWH is Divine above and below.
Her covenant loyalty demands covenant loyalty in return.
She bargains for the life of an entire family, not only herself.
The spies pledge their own lives to the agreement.
Her wall-house becomes an escape route through the window.
She gives the spies the strategy that will save them.
The men begin defining the limits of their oath.
A scarlet cord marks the house where sanctuary must be honored.
Safety is bound to remaining within the protected threshold.
Disclosure would dissolve the agreement.
Rahab accepts the terms and ties the cord before the army arrives.
The spies follow her instructions and evade the search.
They return to Joshua carrying Rahab's intelligence.
They interpret the city's fear as proof of promised victory.

contemplative_note: Rahab is not a prop in someone else's conquest story. She reads power accurately, protects people at risk, negotiates terms, secures her household, and directs the spies' escape. Her courage deserves to remain visible without shame attached to her work or body.
